title: Self-Care & Stabilization Practice Checklist
description: Track your daily self-care practices across the five domains of trauma recovery stabilization. Check off the practices you're currently doing and use this as a gentle guide — not a to-do list that needs to be perfect.
instructions: Review each item and check off the practices you have done this week. Revisit weekly to track your progress. Start with the foundations (safety, sleep, routine, basic needs) before adding more. Even one checked item is meaningful progress.

items.label: I am in a physically safe living environment
I have at least one person I feel safe with
I have set or maintained a boundary that protects my recovery
I have crisis resources saved and accessible (988, therapist number)
I maintained consistent sleep and wake times most days
I followed a calming bedtime routine
I followed a basic daily routine (meals, activities, rest)
I ate regular meals (even if small)
I drank enough water throughout the day
I did some form of gentle movement (walking, stretching, yoga)
I took care of basic hygiene (shower, clean clothes, brushing teeth)
I avoided using alcohol or substances to cope
I practiced a grounding technique (5-4-3-2-1, cold water, breathing)
I allowed myself to feel emotions without judging them
I used a creative outlet (journaling, drawing, music, writing)
I did at least one thing that brought me comfort or small joy
I connected with a safe person (call, text, in person)
I asked for help or support when I needed it
I spent some time outdoors or in nature
I did something that felt meaningful or purposeful

disclaimer: This checklist is for self-care tracking purposes. It is not a clinical tool and does not replace professional mental health support. Healing is not linear — some weeks will be harder than others, and that's okay. If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
